PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 6 Sub-batch 6.2b-i SHIPPED — heatmap-sample shared library + Per Area Report data loader extension (eventLocations + patrolTracks). First sub-batch of Item 2 Page 2 — pure-logic foundation. UI layer (Leaflet client island + leaflet.heat dep + Page 2 RSC + mount) deferred to 6.2b-ii per the locked decomposition. Locked decision "Heatmap Renderer Choice = leaflet.heat plugin + ~250m densification" written to DECISIONS_LOG.md as governance contract for the renderer (rejects density-grid SVG and hybrid mix); governs Per Area Report + ALL future report families that need density overlays.

              NEW packages/shared/src/lib/heatmap-sample/ (4 files, ~210 LOC). HeatLatLng = [number, number, number] tuple type (Leaflet HeatLatLng convention — [lat, lon, weight]); SampleTrackPointsOptions{intervalMeters default 250, weight default 1}. sampleTrackPoints(lineString, options) is a pure haversine-stepping densifier: walks the input GeoJSON LineString segment-by-segment maintaining a cumulative arc-length cursor, emits one point per intervalMeters via linear lat/lon interpolation between straddling vertices. haversineDistanceMeters exported primitive on WGS-84 mean radius 6,371,008.8 m with [0,1] h-clamp before asin for float safety. Edge cases: <2 vertices → []; coincident vertices → [start-flipped]; sub-interval line → [start-flipped]; zero-length intermediate segments skipped; intervalMeters<=0 throws. Linear interpolation deviation vs great-circle at 250m intervals in Philippines lat band is <1cm (documented in JSDoc). Anti-meridian wrap NOT handled (v2 launch tenants all in PH bbox 117-127°E — impossible to traverse). Tests: 17 vitest cases across 6 describe blocks (haversine primitive, degenerate input, output convention, uniform line scaling, multi-segment polyline, options, Philippines tenant-zone sanity).

              MODIFIED apps/web/src/server/per-area-report/get-per-area-report-data.ts (+170 LOC including JSDoc updates). Import sampleTrackPoints + HeatLatLng from @marine-guardian/shared/lib/heatmap-sample; import extractTrackPolyline from @/server/coverage-report/get-coverage-report-data (single source of truth — already exported by 6.1b's loader). TRACK_SAMPLE_INTERVAL_METERS=250 constant. New exported interfaces PerAreaReportEventLocation{lat, lon, eventTypeId} + PerAreaReportPatrolTrack{patrolId, patrolType, sampledPoints: HeatLatLng[]}. PerAreaReportData gains 3 fields lawEnforcementEventLocations + monitoringEventLocations + patrolTracks (parallels existing lawEnforcementBreakdown + monitoringBreakdown nomenclature). buildEventLocations(raw, needle) filters events whose EventType.category matches the needle (same predicate as buildBreakdown) AND whose locationLat/locationLon are finite; co-passes with breakdown build so no extra Event query is issued. buildPatrolTracks(raw) extracts each patrol's track polyline via extractTrackPolyline, runs sampleTrackPoints at TRACK_SAMPLE_INTERVAL_METERS, emits PerAreaReportPatrolTrack rows; patrols with null track / non-LineString geometry / polylines<2 points are skipped (still appear in patrolSummary counts — the heatmap and summary counts diverge intentionally). Events query SELECT extended with locationLat + locationLon. Patrols query SELECT extended with id + track:{select:{trackGeojson:true}}.

              MODIFIED test file (+330 LOC = 7 net new cases in new "Page 2 heatmap data" describe + fidelity fix on the existing "splits patrol summary" mock to include id + track:null per the new SELECT shape). New cases: populate lawEnforcementEventLocations from events with finite lat/lon and matching category (splits monitoring correctly); skip null/NaN coords; skip null-eventType + unmatched-category events from heatmaps; build patrolTracks from materialised LineString tracks (sampledPoints lat/lon in Philippines band, weight=1, trackless patrol still counts in summary); skip patrols with Point/single-vertex/malformed-JSON tracks; verify patrol SELECT includes track relation AND event SELECT includes locationLat/locationLon; regression case for empty arrays.

              MODIFIED packages/shared/package.json (+1 LOC exports entry "./lib/heatmap-sample" — pure JS/TS lib, no new dependencies).

              DECISIONS_LOG.md: new entry "Heatmap Renderer Choice (Phase 8 Batch 6 Sub-batch 6.2b)" — locks leaflet.heat plugin + ~250m densification as the renderer contract for the Per Area Report and ALL future density-overlay reports (Quarterly Report, ad-hoc analytics exports). Rejects custom density-grid SVG renderer (would require +1 shared library + 15-20 algorithm tests + SVG legend component for the same funder-facing output). Rejects hybrid mix (two visual styles + two test patterns + two render-ready paths to sync). Implementation guidance for 6.2b-ii baked into the entry: shared library packages/shared/src/lib/heatmap-sample is the densifier (THIS sub-batch); apps/web/package.json adds leaflet.heat ^0.2.0 + @types/leaflet.heat ^0.2.4 (next sub-batch); new client island per-area-heatmap-map.tsx extends the AreaCoverageMap pattern with a thin HeatLayer wrapper hook using useMap() + L.heatLayer().addTo(map) on mount + map.removeLayer on unmount; variant palettes events=red gradient (red-200 → red-600), tracks=blue gradient (blue-200 → blue-700); legend rendered server-side in the RSC composer as a static gradient bar to avoid hydration mismatch; the MapReadySignal contract from "Coverage Report Page 2 Map Render Strategy" applies unchanged.

              lessons.md: new 🟤 decision entry pointing at the DECISIONS_LOG.md decision lock, capturing the same rationale + implementation guidance + forward-looking gotcha companion (the HeatLayer wrapper hook pattern). Reading order remains 🔴 first → 🟤 second; the new entry sits at the top of the 🟤 stack for the next session to read first when picking up 6.2b-ii.

              **6.2b-ii scope (UI layer):**
              - apps/web/package.json: add `leaflet.heat ^0.2.0` + `@types/leaflet.heat ^0.2.4` runtime deps. No other deps.
              - NEW apps/web/src/app/print-render/[tenantSlug]/[reportType]/[exportId]/components/heat-layer.tsx (~50 LOC) — thin Client wrapper hook component. Uses useMap() from react-leaflet + useEffect to L.heatLayer(points, options).addTo(map) on mount and map.removeLayer(heat) on unmount. Props: points: HeatLatLng[] + variant: 'events'|'tracks' (drives palette gradient). Cleanup on prop change to avoid layered duplicates between re-renders.
              - NEW apps/web/src/app/print-render/[tenantSlug]/[reportType]/[exportId]/components/per-area-heatmap-map.tsx (~200 LOC) — Client island extending the AreaCoverageMap pattern: MapContainer + TileLayer + AutoFitBounds (computed from all incoming heat points + boundary if optionally passed) + <HeatLayer variant="events" points={…}/> + <HeatLayer variant="tracks" points={…}/> + MapReadySignal. Per the locked "Coverage Report Page 2 Map Render Strategy" the MapReadySignal contract applies unchanged — waits for tile load + paint flush before flipping window.__renderReady=true for Puppeteer.
